channel.title: Progress on The Tradewinds Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Progress on The Tradewinds Brief

item.title: Uncle Ramesh: Exxon Investing in We Future, Ali Preparing We for Global Storm, and PPP Government Delivering Despite Opposition Noise
item.description:
Morning to all my people. Uncle Ramesh here, cup of Mauby in hand, looking out over the backdam and thinking about what the papers saying this Sunday. Let me walk you through it the way it really is, not the way the opposition vexers trying to paint it.

1. Ram and his “amateurish” talk

So Christopher Ram go on radio and call the government “amateurish” for not ring-fencing the Stabroek Block. You know what Uncle Ramesh think? Easy to talk big when you not the one at the table with Exxon.

item.title: The Guyanese Horizon — March 2026: The City and the Streets
item.description: The Guyanese Horizon — March 2026. Georgetown is changing faster than at any point since independence. New highways, contested streets, a growing skyline. What does the capital look like when a country strikes oil and starts spending?

item.title: 📊 Weekly Progress Report: February 5-11, 2026
item.description: This week’s government achievements: Budget 2026 approvals rolling through Committee of Supply, Gas-to-Energy at 68%, new Amerindian hostel funded, recycling centre launched, and 30 bad contractors blacklisted.

item.title: 📈 Weekly Progress Report: January 8-15, 2026
item.description: A factual summary of PPP/C government accomplishments for the week of January 8-15, 2026. No commentary, just documented achievements and initiatives.
